detaileddescription.para: When timespec_add_sec() is called with a pointer to the time interval (timespec) and a number of seconds
Then the time interval value is increased by the requested number
When timespec_add_msec() is called with a pointer to the time interval (timespec) and a number of miliseconds
Then the time interval value is increased by the requested number calls timespec_add()
When timespec_add_usec() is called with a pointer to the time interval (timespec) and a number of microseconds
Then the time interval value is increased by the requested number calls timespec_add()
When timespec_add_nsec() is called with a pointer to the time interval (timespec) and a number of nanoseconds
Then the time interval value is increased by the requested number calls timespec_add()
When timespec_sub() is called with pointers to the time interval (timespec) and another time interval (timespec) equal to zero
Then the time interval value is not changed
When timespec_sub() is called with pointers to the time interval (timespec) and another time interval (timespec) less than one second
Then the time interval value is decreased by the requested value
When timespec_sub() is called with a pointer to the time interval (timespec) and another time interval (timespec) greater than one second
Then the time interval value is decreased by the requested value
When timespec_sub_sec() is called with a pointer to the time interval (timespec) and a number of seconds
Then the time interval value is decreased by the requested number
When timespec_sub_msec() is called with a pointer to the time interval (timespec) and a number of miliseconds
Then the time interval value is decreased by the requested number calls timespec_sub()
When timespec_sub_usec() is called with a pointer to the time interval (timespec) and a number of microseconds
Then the time interval value is decreased by the requested number calls timespec_sub()
When timespec_sub_nsec() is called with a pointer to the time interval (timespec) and a number of nanoseconds
Then the time interval value is decreased by the requested number calls timespec_sub()
When timespec_diff_sec() is called with pointers to two different time intervals (timespec), difference between them is greater than or equal to one second
Then the difference in seconds between the time intervals is returned
When timespec_diff_sec() is called with pointers to two different time intervals (timespec), difference between them is less than one second
Then 0 is returned
When timespec_diff_msec() is called with pointers to two different time intervals (timespec), difference between them is greater than or equal to one second
Then the difference in miliseconds is returned
When timespec_diff_msec() is called with pointers to two different time intervals (timespec), difference between them is less than one second
Then the difference in miliseconds is returned
When timespec_diff_usec() is called with pointers to two different time intervals (timespec), difference between them is greater than or equal to one second
Then the difference in microseconds is returned
When timespec_diff_usec() is called with pointers to two different time intervals (timespec), difference between them is less than one second
Then the difference in microseconds is returned
When timespec_diff_nsec() is called with pointers to two different time intervals (timespec), difference between them is greater than or equal to one second
Then the difference in nanoseconds is returned
When timespec_diff_nsec() is called with pointers to two different time intervals (timespec), difference between them is less than one second
Then the difference is returned
When timespec_cmp() is called with a null pointer and a pointer to the time interval (timespec)
Then a value greater than 0 is returned
When timespec_cmp() is called with a pointer to the time interval (timespec) and a null pointer
Then a value less than 0 is returned
When timespec_cmp() is called with two pointers to the same time interval (timespec)
Then 0 is returned
When timespec_cmp() is called with two pointers to time intervals (timespec) with equal values
Then 0 is returned
When timespec_cmp() is called with two pointers to time intervals (timespec), second one is less than the first
Then a value greater than 0 is returned
When timespec_cmp() is called with two pointers to time intervals (timespec), second one is greater than the first
Then a value less than 0 is returned

briefdescription.para: This file is part of the CANopen Library Unit Test Suite.

detaileddescription.para:

Unit Test Suite was developed under a programme of, and funded by, the European Space Agency.
